user_query:
Kia ora! I run a small, hobby website about NZ wasps and I’d like help improving on-page SEO for better organic CTR. Please work entirely from the local files provided and produce the outputs described below.

Inputs:
- Metrics CSV: input/metrics/pages.csv
- Source pages (HTML): input/site_pages/*.html

Task:
1) Compute a priority score for each page in input/metrics/pages.csv using this formula:
   priority_score = impressions * max(0, 0.06 - ctr) + 300*(meta_desc_length < 110) + 200*(avg_position >= 10) + (staleness_days / 30)
   Notes:
   - ctr is a decimal in [0,1].
   - meta_desc_length is the character length of the current meta description in the corresponding HTML file (0 if missing).
   - For each row, match the HTML by slug at input/site_pages/{slug}.html.

2) Create a ranked CSV at output/priority/priority_ranking.csv with these columns, sorted by descending priority_score:
   slug, title, priority_score (rounded to 2 decimals), ctr, impressions, avg_position, staleness_days, meta_desc_length, is_short (true/false for meta_desc_length < 110), selected_for_update ("yes" for the top 2 pages by priority_score, else "no").

3) For the two pages with selected_for_update = "yes":
   - Read the existing <title> and <meta name="description"> from the input HTML to capture the old values.
   - Create updated files at output/site_pages/{slug}.html with only these changes in the <head>:
     • Write a new, concise <title> (<= 60 chars) and a new <meta name="description"> (140–160 chars).
     • Use NZ English, reflect the page’s existing H1/body content (don’t invent new facts or species), and include a clear relevance to New Zealand (e.g., “NZ” or “New Zealand”).
     • Keep the rest of the HTML (especially <body>) unchanged.
     • If a meta description is missing, add one; if present, replace its content.

4) Compose a short status report at output/seo_update_summary.md that includes:
   - Overview: a brief paragraph on what you did and why (context: NZ wasp site, improving CTR and clarity for readers).
   - Changes: for each updated page, list slug, old title → new title, old meta description → new meta description, and the priority_score used to justify the change.
   - Next steps: list the next two highest-priority pages that were not updated, with one sentence each on why (e.g., short/missing meta, high impressions with low CTR, stale content) referencing metrics from the CSV.

Assumptions & constraints:
- Do not use the internet; rely only on the provided files.
- All ranking and selection must come from the given formula and inputs.
- The status report and ranking must be consistent with the input metrics and the updated HTML outputs.

Deliverables:
- output/priority/priority_ranking.csv
- output/site_pages/{slug}.html files for the two selected pages
- output/seo_update_summary.md

Let me know if anything in the inputs is unclear. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
